feed.title: SAP Community - Artificial Intelligence
feed.subtitle: Artificial Intelligence Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP BDC Demo Environment
entry.content:
Hello All, 

Since the BDC trial version is now available, I’d like to create a demo using it. Could you please guide me on how to enable or build the content? Also, is it possible to showcase a demo using standard content, considering that the source systems are not properly maintained? I’m looking for any way to effectively pitch it to clients. I’m exploring options to work exclusively within BDC AI/ML part. The practice source systems are not properly maintained, making it impractical to establish connections with them. However, intelligent applications or insight apps require both source and target systems to be connected in order to enable the content. Are there any materials or documents available that can guide the implementation of Databricks AI/ML on raw data within an SAP BDC environment?

entry.title: Need information on different SAP AI modules/Resources
entry.content:
I am preparing for the SAP AI certification and went through all the relevant course material.  I still need clarification on some concepts.  I have put my understanding of different SAP AI resources with an example. Could someone clarify if my understanding is correct ?

SAP AI Module Primary Role What It Does Examples

SAP AI CoreManages end-to-end lifecycle of any ML model- Train, deploy, monitor containerized models- Define YAML pipelines, executables, secrets🔹 Train an MRP delay prediction model using TensorFlow🔹 Monitor PO exception forecasting
SAP Generative AI HubConnects to external LLMs via an abstraction layer- Prompt execution- Orchestration- Safety filters- Document grounding🔹 Ask GPT-4 to summarize blocked invoices in S/4HANA🔹 Use Claude to explain a supply issue
SAP AI LaunchpadCentral UI for managing all AI assets (Core + GenAI)- Deployment dashboard- Manage pipelines, prompts, templates, secrets🔹 See status of ML model runs🔹 Manage GenAI Hub prompts and results in one place
SAP AI FoundationInfrastructure layer that powers Core & GenAI- Resource orchestration (e.g., CPU, GPU)- Security, observability, scaling🔹 Supports storage access to datasets in Azure blob🔹 Enables auth and runtime allocation
SAP AI Business ServicesPre-built APIs for common AI tasks- Pre-trained services like: ▪ Document Information Extraction ▪ Service Ticket Intelligence🔹 Auto-extract invoice fields from PDF🔹 Categorize helpdesk ticket based on past text data
SAP HANA Vector EngineEmbedding & vector search for RAG or semantic search- Stores embeddings- Enables Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines🔹 Query internal policies grounded to document embeddings🔹 Match customer queries to SOPs
SAP BTP SDK for Generative AI (Python)SDK to call GenAI Hub/Orchestration APIs from code- Define LLM, prompt, and deployment in Python- Access orchestration APIs🔹 Call GPT from a script to validate MRP output🔹 Automate prompt evaluation over vendor data

entry.title: Can Joule AI Make SAP Readiness Check & Process Insights Smarter?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m Tommy, an intern at SAP. As part of my internship, I’ve been given the research topic:

                  "How to utilise Joule to interpret SAP tools such as Readiness Check and Signavio Process Insights"

After discussions with the original topic proposer, I’ve refined this to:

                    "Leveraging Joule AI for immediate, context-aware interpretation of SAP tools such as Readiness Check and Process                                  Insights outputs through integrated or standalone models."

Current ideas under exploration:

Potential role of AI (Joule):

                     Integrated approach – Joule embedded directly in tools for live Q&A and guidance.

                     Standalone approach – Central Joule portal where exported tool data is uploaded for AI-generated summaries.

Industry & process-specific insights:

                     Could Joule be trained using Signavio Process Explorer as a knowledge source?

Customer-specific process learning:

                     Could Joule learn a customer’s business processes over time to adjust tool benchmark thresholds?

                     For example, if a process is flagged as problematic by a benchmark but is actually fine for a specific customer, could                                 Joule adapt to avoid false alarms?

I’m not looking to start a conversation and see if this direction aligns with others’ perspectives and see if anyone has experience in the topic.

Would love to hear your thoughts!

entry.title: SAP Business AI – Driving the Next Wave of Enterprise Innovation
entry.content:
Why It Matters

AI is no longer a distant concept—it’s transforming how businesses operate today. Generative AI alone is projected to add up to $4.4 trillion in global productivity within the next five years. For SAP customers, who generate 87% of the world’s commerce, the impact of embedding AI directly into core business processes is both immediate and profound.

Key Highlights:

Joule – The AI Copilot

  • Acts as SAP’s unified front end, enabling users to work in natural language.

  • Delivers context-aware insights, transaction execution, and navigation across SAP apps.

  • Boosts efficiency by up to 80% for employees.

  • Coordinates specialized AI agents for complex tasks (e.g., dispute resolution, sustainability planning).

  • Integrates with Microsoft Copilot for Office 365 for seamless cross-platform experiences.

Embedded AI – Innovation at Scale

  • AI features are built directly into SAP’s cloud solutions (ERP, Supply Chain, HCM, CRM, Procurement, etc.).

  • Use cases already improving business outcomes:

    • Henkel → Faster, data-driven decisions with AI in SAP Analytics Cloud.

    • FC Bayern Munich → Accelerated recruiting with generative AI in SAP SuccessFactors.

    • Gibson → Boosted email revenue by 50% through AI-powered customer engagement.

  • Over 100+ new AI features released in 2024, with continuous quarterly rollouts.

AI Foundation – Building Custom Solutions

  • Part of SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).

  • Provides developers with reusable AI services, lifecycle management, and access to leading LLMs (OpenAI, Google, AWS, Meta, etc.).

  • Offers deep integration with SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine and SAP Knowledge Graph for contextualized business data.

  • Enables low-data AI innovation, such as invoice due date predictions or sales order auto-filling .

Responsible AI at SAP

  • Commitment to ethics, privacy, and security:

    • No partner or third-party models are trained on customer data.

    • AI ethics embedded in governance via SAP’s AI Ethics Steering Committee.

    • Guardrails against misuse, bias, and security risks.

  • Alignment with UNESCO AI ethics standards and EU AI Act compliance .

SAP’s vision is AI-first and suite-first:

  • AI copilots like Joule will become hyper-personalized.

  • More AI-driven automation across finance, HR, supply chain, and sustainability.

The future is already here. With SAP Business AI, organizations can run better, innovate faster, and empower people to focus on what truly matters.

Explore more:

  • SAP Business AI Feature Catalog

  • Learning Journeys

entry.title: Error
entry.content: error

entry.title: Finding SAP Note using AI
entry.content:
The Challenge Every Basis Administrator Knows

If you’ve worked in SAP Basis, you know the scenario well:
A transport fails, a short dump appears in ST22, or a background job terminates.

The next step? Hunting through SAP Notes in the Launchpad.

This process is often:

  • Time-consuming, with hundreds of possible Notes.

  • Keyword-driven, meaning you need to guess the right search terms.

  • Trial-and-error, as many Notes don’t exactly match the error in your system.

The result is hours of lost productivity, delayed fixes, and frustrated teams.

Today’s SAP Notes search is powerful, but it depends heavily on manual filtering. Notes can be technical, fragmented, or reference outdated versions of components. Even experienced Basis administrators often spend too much time narrowing down the “right” Note.

At the same time, SAP landscapes are becoming more complex, spanning S/4HANA, cloud integrations, DB migrations, and hybrid infrastructures. This complexity only increases the challenge of quickly mapping system errors to the correct Note.

Where AI Can Make a Difference

This is where Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Natural Language Processing (NLP) can transform the way we work with SAP Notes.

Imagine a system that could:

  • Read your error log automatically (from ST22, SM21, STMS, or job logs).

  • Understand the context of the error beyond keywords (e.g., system version, module, DB type).

  • Recommend the top 3 SAP Notes most relevant to fixing that exact error.

  • Learn from history. If a Note solved the issue for your team in the past, it would prioritize that next time.

This would save Basis teams valuable hours, reduce downtime, and make troubleshooting far more efficient.

How It Could Work in Practice

  1. Log Capture: System errors, dumps, or transport logs are collected (e.g., via Solution Manager or Focused Run).

  2. AI/NLP Engine: An AI model trained on historical SAP error messages and Notes embeddings interprets the log text.

  3. Notes Recommendation: The system suggests a short, ranked list of relevant Notes.

  4. Feedback Loop: Administrators confirm whether the suggested Note fixed the issue, improving recommendations over time.

This approach transforms troubleshooting from manual search to AI-powered recommendation.

AI-powered Note recommendation is just the beginning. Over time, such a system could evolve into an intelligent assistant that:

  • Correlates SAP + OS + DB logs for cross-layer analysis.

  • Validates system refreshes and upgrades by comparing logs before and after.

  • Predicts risks in transports or background jobs before they fail.

This vision aligns with SAP’s own push toward intelligent operations and embedded AI, but with a strong focus on the Basis layer, where today much of the work is still manual.

Final Thoughts

By applying AI and NLP to SAP Notes, Basis teams can move from reactive troubleshooting to proactive, intelligent support.

This is an area where I believe there is huge potential for innovation, research, and where every Basis professional can benefit.

I’m exploring this further and would love to connect with others interested in AI + Basis.

entry.title: Unable to Complete Practical Test- Applying the Value Story of SAP Business AI
entry.content:
Hello All,

I am planning to give the SAP certification exam

SAP Certified Associate- Positioning SAP Business AI Solutions as part of SAP Business Suite, however, I am unable to complete  the practical test Applying the Value Story of SAP Business AI in the learning journey.

I have followed the steps as given in the video in the learning journey (Customer Return Management with SAP Business AI in SAP S/4 Hana Cloud) however still not able to complete the practical test as a result of which I am not able to give the final exam.

I have attached the screenshots for your reference, request your help for the same.

Kind Regards

Atul

entry.title: How to properly configure SAP CAP MCP Server so Codex can detect it?
entry.content:
I'm trying to configure Codex to detect and use the SAP CAP MCP Server, but Codex keeps saying that no MCP server is running, even though I can start it manually and see it listening on a port.

Here is what I tried so far:

mcp.json

{
"servers": {
"cds-mcp": {
"command": "npx",
"args": ["-y", "@cap-js/mcp-server"],
"env": {},
"type": "stdio"
}
},
"inputs": []
}

 

The SAP CAP MCP server appears to run correctly (screenshot attached), but Codex still reports:

config.toml (Codex)

[tools.mcp-server-cap]
type = "mcp"
command = "cds-mcp"
args = ["--port", "4005"]

 

Question

Has anyone managed to configure Codex with the CAP MCP server?
Is there something obvious I'm missing. Perhaps related to TCP vs STDIO mode or how Codex expects MCP servers to be declared?

Any help or working example would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: How can GenAI be effectively used in SAP S/4HANA implementation and support projects?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I would like to understand how Generative AI (GenAI) can be practically applied within the SAP ecosystem, especially in S/4HANA implementation, migration, and support projects.

Specifically, I’m interested in insights on:

  • Using GenAI for requirements analysis and documentation

  • Automating test case creation and test data preparation

  • Supporting ABAP development, code review, and optimization

  • Enhancing functional consulting (FI, MM, SD, etc.) through AI-driven analysis

  • Improving incident management and ticket resolution in AMS/support scenarios

  • Any SAP-native tools or integrations (e.g., SAP Joule, SAP BTP + AI services)

I’d appreciate real-world examples, best practices, limitations to be aware of, and recommendations on where GenAI adds the most value in SAP projects.

Thank you in advance for sharing your experiences and suggestions.

Best regards,
Juveria

entry.title: AI powered products - Alignment with SAP
entry.content: Hi, I am designing and developing AI powered products specifically for SAP. I want to align with SAP to make that these products don´t already exist, and that they can eventually be integrated into SAP. The goal is to add value to SAP´s existing product portfolio, leveraging on Artificial Intelligence. Who can I contact in SAP?

entry.title: Technical RFC: Building an "Auditable Autopilot" for Material Shortages – Where is the real Excel He
entry.content:
Hi everyone,
I’m an AI developer in Germany architecting a "Human-in-the-loop" agent to automate exception handling for discrete manufacturers (Mittelstand).
The goal is to reduce the 4+ hours of manual coordination that happens when a supplier is late or a carrier delay occurs, leading to potential line stops. We are focusing on a safe, auditable "exception autopilot" model rather than a generic "digital brain."
I have a few "reality check" questions for the consultants and experts here. I need brutal honesty so I don't build a product that fails in a real German factory environment:
  1. The 'Shadow' System: When a delay hits, does the rescheduling happen in SAP (e.g., MD04/CO06) immediately, or do planners usually run a 'Shadow Excel' for 1-2 days first to figure out the impact?
  2. The Data Gap: What’s the one piece of information that is never in SAP (e.g., real-time carrier GPS, unstructured email updates) that forces a human to manually 'bridge' the gap?
  3. The 'Green Button' Barrier: If an AI drafted the SAP update and the supplier email, what is the #1 reason a German Plant Manager would not press 'Approve'? (e.g., legal liability, lack of audit trail, trust issues).
  4. On-Premise vs. Cloud: For a mid-sized manufacturer, would the IT department demand this system runs 100% on-premise for data sovereignty, or is a highly controlled cloud connection (like SAP BTP) acceptable now in 2026?
Looking forward to the insights from the community so we can build something that actually solves the real pain points.
Thanks in advance for your expertise!

entry.title: Question: Role-based access control in a chatbot using SAP Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I’m preparing a pre-sales presentation for a real use case with SAP Generative AI Hub, and I want to address a security topic that customers typically ask about.

I have been searching for quite a long time for official documentation, concrete examples, or direct answers on this, but I have not been able to find a single clear reference that explains it conclusively.

My question is conceptual: when a chatbot consumes a model via Generative AI Hub and needs to retrieve business information (for example from SAP S/4HANA), how can we ensure that the responses respect the end user’s roles and authorization levels, and that no information is exposed beyond what the user is allowed to access?

I would especially appreciate:

  • Official SAP references (documentation, guides, or notes) that describe the recommended approach for access control and governance in these scenarios.

  • Expert recommendations on how to explain, at a high level, that end-to-end user authorizations are preserved (for example concepts such as identity/principal propagation), without going into configuration details.

Thank you for any guidance or references you can share!

PD:
I've done https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/solving-business-problems-using-sap-s-generative-ai-hub
And still, i'm not 100% sure on how roles,governance and security works.

entry.title: How AI Is Improving Clinical Communication and Compliance in Life Sciences
entry.content:
Clinical communication across life sciences has become increasingly complex—spanning clinical trials, pharmacovigilance, regulatory submissions, and post-marketing safety. Teams are expected to process growing volumes of data while maintaining accuracy, traceability, and regulatory compliance.

Artificial Intelligence is helping address these challenges by improving how clinical and safety information is captured, structured, and shared. AI-driven automation can streamline adverse event intake, normalize data from multiple sources, support compliant documentation, and improve visibility across clinical and safety workflows.

In regulated environments, AI works best when combined with strong domain expertise and governance. Rather than replacing human judgment, it enhances consistency, reduces manual effort, and supports inspection readiness throughout the drug lifecycle.

I’d love to hear from the community:

Where have you seen AI deliver the most value in clinical or safety communication?

What barriers still exist when adopting AI in regulated life sciences settings?

Looking forward to learning from your experiences.

entry.title: AI with SAP
entry.content:
Core concept is as follows. In the age of AI, the most unstructured data/information is so well organised. But the most organised data in RDBMS and/or SAP systems is still opaque to business users who do not have expertise on SAP Functional and SAP Technical Side.

PrimeAISAP is a set of projects being worked to make business users TALK to SAP using natural language for all their informational/decisional needs on day to day basis.

This started as a need to one of our auto component manufacturing customer's CEO 10 years ago wanting to talk to SAP and get all answers back in natural language. We did some work on automating a layer on top of BO/SAP and text-to-speech and speech-to-text layer. The project was not successful for various reasons, but main reason is it lacked LLM like thinking. It also lacked ability to talk on variety of knowledge sources. This is what we are working on #iitb #akashmavle @AIwithSAP @aiandsap

entry.title: Joule Studio: Why is build_default required if standard plan exists?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I’m currently setting up Joule Studio in SAP BTP and reviewing the prerequisites.

According to the documentation, Joule Studio requires an entitlement for SAP Build Process Automation with the service plan “build_default”.

However, in my subaccount I already have the “standard” (standard-application) plan assigned, which provides full production capabilities for SAP Build Process Automation (workflows, RPA, monitoring, etc.).

From my understanding:

  • The standard plan offers full functionality for productive use

  • The build_default plan seems to provide a more limited or base-level feature set

This raises a concern, as introducing or switching to build_default could potentially limit existing capabilities.

My questions are:

  1. Is the build_default plan technically required for Joule Studio, even if the standard plan is already available?

  2. Is there a dependency or integration requirement that specifically relies on build_default?

  3. Can both plans coexist without impacting existing Build Process Automation scenarios?

  4. What is the recommended entitlement setup for using Joule Studio in a productive BTP environment?

Any clarification or best practices would be highly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Regarding Claude Mythos
entry.content:
We are startup which focuses on disruptive thinking, Advanced MCP Agents, Agentic AI Agents and other modern AI tools to build platforms on BTP Architecture.

We are planning to go for Silver partnership. Claude Mythos, which is one of the most powerful Model which has been tested in closed group of top tech companies. Capability of this model is it can hack any strongest tech company across the globe. Anthropic has shared this with 50 top most companies.

My doubt is whether SAP is in that 50 companies or not

Reference link:
https://anthropic.com/claude-mythos-preview-risk-report

entry.title: Do you think your current ERP setup supports or limits your AI initiatives?
entry.content: I feel like ERP can either really help or quietly get in the way of AI. Curious how it’s working for you.

entry.title: At what point does ERP complexity start affecting business performance?
entry.content: At some point ERP complexity just starts slowing everything down… wondering when that usually hits for others.

entry.title: Do you think your current ERP setup supports or limits your AI initiatives?
entry.content: Does your ERP actually help when you try to do something with AI, or does it end up getting in the way?

entry.title: At what point does ERP complexity start affecting business performance?
entry.content: When does ERP complexity stop being manageable and start becoming a real problem for the business? When do you actually start feeling it in daily operations, like things getting slower or more annoying than they should be?
